transcript: This is Dick calling from UPS. Hey, I’ve been trying to bring a box to your, uh, place, there, for the last three days but you guys are making so much noise you don’t hear me!
There’s nobody at the house between eight and four.
—comes to the door, you best listen up; d’you understand?
I don’t know—I don’t know what you’re talking about. I don’t know what---w—who sent, the boss or what?
When you tell me to listen up, that’s when you sh—shut me out, okay?
I need a credit card payment on this thing: C.O.D. $242.
$242? Just return it, because I don’t know what that is; I don’t know who it is; I don’t know—
You don’t know who I am; you don’t know anything about me—return it!
Okay, but, th—okay, then what do you want me to do? It’s not my box. What do you want me to do?—
—Read me your credit card number and I’ll just leave it by your door—
—I’m not gonna give you my credit card number, ‘cause it’s not my box—I don’t even know what—you can’t even tell me what it is, so how am I supposed to know what I’m paying for?!
Goose Purse?! I don’t even know what that is!
Well, it’s none of my concern—
—And why d—why—why isn’t it—why isn’t it returnable?
I’ve worked—I work with UPS! I work with UPS! You guys can return it, okay? So don’t tell me you cannot return it!
You drag us out there three times in the pourin’ rain and then you say you don’t want it? Nah, it’s a little late for that!
Yo—no no no no no no! I’ve never ordered anything from there, so I don’t know what you’re talking about!—

—So I’m not gonna pay $242—Okay! This is—is the first I’ve ever heard of this company—Goose Purse?! It’s the first I’ve ever heard from you! And you call telling me that I’m outrageous, disrespecting me! Don’t—don’t—d—don’t talk to me like that! You don’t know who I am, okay? You don’t know anything about me for you to disrespect me like that. And what’s your name, again?

Dick?! Okay, wha—Listen, Dick: You don’t know me t—to d—disrespect me like that, okay? If—if there’s nobody there to sign the package, then you know what? You can return it back, okay? Nobody talks to me like that!
Just read the credit card number and I’ll leave it at your door.
Oh—no! I’m not gonna give you a credit card. Do you not know [unclear] your shit-eating at $242!
What is Goose Purse dot—I don’t even know what that is! How did I—stuff I didn’t order things of a card [sic]. ‘Cause I got the card, a while back.

No, I’m nat full of excuses! Why am I full of excuses, because I’m telling you that I did not order anything from there? Obviously, this is the first time I ever heard UPS disrespect somebody th—this way, ever! I know the manager! I know them all! So don’t tell me that—that—you cannot ret—return the box; it’s not an option And yes, it is an option! ‘Cause I did not order anything from there!
Okay, okay, what’s your last name? Wh—Dick what?
—Dick what? No no no no no! I’m serious! What’s your last name?

No no n—n—no! D—d—don’t talk to me like that! I’m asking you, what’s your last name, ‘cause you know you’re being wrong! I’ll call back and I’ll talk to a main main manager and I’ll call a regional manager and I’ll talk to him. And I’ll tell him I got a d—really disrespectful call! This is the first I’ve heard of it, okay? Y—you guys are obnoxious! You don’t know me! I’m i—I’m in fantasy land?! I make three times what you make! You just d—you don’t know who you’re talking to, okay? So just relax, relax! You can return that box! And it’s not even about the money; it’s about the way you’re talking to me—the way you’re talking to me like I owe you! No! I don’t owe you anything, okay? I don’t owe anybody anything that I don’t know what the hell it is! Y---you can’t tell me anything else and you can’t tell me you cannot return it—you can’t not—tell me that I h—have to pay for it!
Okay, I—I—I don’t mind $242! Believe me: I have the money! But don’t talk to me like that! I’m not gonna give you the credit card! Have somebody else call me!
You need to turn around, go home, wait by the door, and listen up!

No, I’m not gonna turn around! Listen, fucker, I’m not gonna turn around, because you know what? Because you say so! Because UPS says so, I’m supposed to skip my job and go pick up a $242 box that I have no idea what it is? Because I don’t know who in the heck ordered it! And you’re telling me I have to turn around?! You need to be in fantasy land! You know what? I have a life besides waiting for UPS for something to deliver that I did not order! Right now, I’m not gonna turn around and go home and pick up a stupid box at $242 that I did not order!—
—Just read me the credit card number!—

—[unclear], so—I’m not gonna read you no goddamn credit card number! Have somebody else call me that’s gonna t—talk to me like I’m a damn human being, okay? ‘Cause you have—obviously have a big customer service problem! Huge customer service problem! Okay, so, I need to talk to somebody else, because this is bullshit! Now you’ve pissed me off! I’m not gonna turn around and go back home o—out of our way just to go pick up a—a UPS box ‘cause you said so! This is bullshit! Do—do—don’t talk shit about my house! Don’t talk smack about anything in my life! You don’t know me! You don’t know anything about me! I’m telling you, I’m not gonna fuckin’ read you my card number! Do you not get it?! How am I gonna give you my card number over the pho—You think I’m retarded?! [unclear] UPS!
Lady, go home, wait for my call!—
—No, fucker! Fucker, listen: You shut the fuck up and listen, now, ‘cause now you’re getting me fuckin’ pissed off, okay? And n—I’m not in the mood to listen to you! How do I know you’re UPS?
Because I worked here for 12 years, that’s how!

Okay—o—o—listen, okay! And I’m psychic? I’m supposed to know that? The fact? The fact is I’m supposed to know that? I’ve worked with the UPS for ten years and nobody’s ever asked me for a credit card unless I call them! Okay, you’re calling me asking for a cr’card for two—for $242 that I don’t know nothing about? And I’m supposed to read you my credit card number?! Y’think I’m retarded?! No, fucker; you’re the retarded one ‘cause if give people a credit card numbers [sic] over your phone without knowing if they are UPS or not then you’re the retarded one, okay?! In fact, you give—you give me my social security number over the phone—it’s not gonna happen! So if you want me to give a credit card number, you have somebody else call me that can identify that you are UPS. Give me your last name or I—things are gonna get very ugly, okay? I’ll tell you that right now. ‘Cause UPS has been at—house every day and they drop outside s—and they pick up and drop off! So I don’t know what the hell you’re talking about!
What’s the expiration date on your Visa?
No, I don’t think so! I don’t think so! You’re retarded; you are retarded if you think I’m gonna give you my credit card!—

—Jesus Christ! Are you stupid?! Are you stupid?—You’re asking me—I need—me [sic] credit card number over the phone? How many stupid people do you get to do this for you when you treat ‘em like this? Threatening them and stu— What are you—do, put me in jail ‘cause I’m—give you my credit card number?! [unclear]. There is no noise in my house!; we have no dogs in our house! Okay, so, if you tell me either go to my house, obviously you’re going to the wrong house; obviously you’re talking to the wrong person: H—has that crossed your mind? Has that crossed your mind?

Oh, you’re not acting like one!; you sure as hell are not acting like one!—Start acting like one, then, if you’re a professional! A customer service manager does not talk like that to customers, okay? Mister, stop calling me lady, okay? I’ll tell you w—this much: you are not a professional, and you know what? You don’t even know who you’re talking to! Stop blowing up my phone, okay? I’m telling you right now! I’m not gonna talk to you; I’m not gonna give you my credit card over the phone!
I’m gonna put your box in the dumpster!
Th—yeah, I don’t care, so go ‘head! I’m not paying for it! So, you’re gonna go ahead and throw it in—dumpster. How do you like that?
I need your credit card number PDQ!

—I don’t care! It’s—it’s [unclear] fucking—it’s—answer—listen to me! What’s my name?! Why can’t you give me my name if you’re so sure it’s my package?! Give me a tracking number and I’ll find out where it’s coming from!
Your name’s Mud as far as UPS is concerned!
—give me a tracking number and I’ll find out where it’s coming from!
I ain’t gonna give you anything ’til you give me a credit card number!
Okay, y—you know what? I ain’t gonna give you shit either! How’s that? How’s that? [unclear]—
—No, I’m not gonna fucking turn around; I’m at work! How am I gonna turn around and go and pick up stupid fucking package at $242! [unclear]—
—How ‘bout I just open the box, help myself—
—Yeah, help yourself for [unclear]! Can I talk to your supervisor?
About your attitude! Can I talk to your supervisor now?!
I’m driving right now; I’m drivin’ a truck!

Okay, then you know what? Just answer me—transfer over into the office. You think I’m retarded or something! But you know what? People like you are the ones that—making yourselves look like fools, because you don’t even know what my name is! I n—I know y—you transfer me over to the office and—have a supervisor talk to me. But you know you’re talking shit!
